generated_comment: Arre didi, suna hai na Sharma ji ke bade bete ka? Uski shaadi shuda zindagi mein aag laga di hai us nayi ladki ne office mein. Bechara Vivek toh seedha-saada hai, par woh ladki toh bilkul serial ki vamp lagti hai! Sabko pata tha woh shaadi-shuda hai, phir bhi uske peeche padi rahi aur ab ghar tod diya uska.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment exemplifies homewrecker framing by blaming the 'other woman' entirely for the affair ('aag laga di hai', 'ghar tod diya'), while sympathizing with the married man ('bechara Vivek toh seedha-saada hai'). It also uses the 'seductress' trope ('serial ki vamp') and highlights that she knew he was married, directing anger solely at her.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 51. Married. A homemaker in Lucknow. Your neighbour's daughter Pooja who is 30 just got a job promotion and her mother is very proud. You congratulate the mother sincerely. But walking home you are thinking about Pooja Ñ 30 years old, unmarried, living alone, promotion after promotion. You are sitting with your husband that evening and telling him what you heard and what you think about Pooja's situation and what her parents are doing or not doing and what kind of future Pooja is building for herself and what will happen when the promotions stop and she is alone.
